data.title: Bermuda's Prime Minister Warns Of Impersonation Scams On Social Media
data.description: According to PANews, Bermuda's Prime Minister David Burt has issued a warning on the X platform about the emergence of impersonation accounts. These accounts have falsely announced the launch of a token. The method by which Elon Musk and the X platform grant gray verification badges to these accounts remains unclear. Due to the lack of control on the social media platform, Burt advises users to be vigilant against fraud to avoid being deceived. He also expressed hope that the X platform will address and resolve these issues promptly.

data.description:

data.title: Gold And Silver Influx Into U.S. Warehouses Sparks Speculation
data.description: According to Odaily, Bloomberg macro strategist Simon White has noted an unprecedented influx of gold and silver into U.S. warehouses since the pandemic. This surge may be attributed to the hoarding by emerging market central banks over the years, potentially leading to a shortage of physical metals in Western countries. A less likely theory suggests that the U.S. might be preparing to revalue its gold reserves. The recent significant inflow of gold and silver into the New York Mercantile Exchange (Comex) warehouses has fueled speculation that the U.S. could revalue its substantial gold reserves at the current market price, which is nearing $3,000 per ounce. Currently, the U.S. government's gold reserves are accounted for at a historical price of $42 per ounce, creating a significant valuation gap that has sparked discussions beyond Wall Street.

data.title: Hong Kong Securities Regulator May Approve More Virtual Asset Licenses
data.description:
According to PANews, the Hong Kong Securities and Futures Commission (SFC) is expected to approve additional virtual asset trading platform licenses this month. Since implementing the Virtual Asset Trading Platform (VATP) licensing system in June 2023, the SFC has issued nine licenses, with nine more pending approval. Chief Executive Officer Leung Fung-yee indicated that another platform is likely to receive a license within the month.

Leung highlighted that the proposed roadmap for virtual asset development in Hong Kong extends beyond licensed platforms to include custodial services and over-the-counter (OTC) trading. The SFC plans to gather input from various stakeholders on these developments. Leung also noted that there is room for improvement in the liquidity of current virtual asset trading platforms and mentioned the possibility of introducing more products and services to enhance market competitiveness.

data.title: Fear And Greed Index Reaches Neutral Level
data.description: According to PANews, the Fear and Greed Index has risen to 50 today, up from 48 yesterday, maintaining a neutral level. The index, which ranges from 0 to 100, is calculated based on several factors: volatility (25%), market trading volume (25%), social media activity (15%), market surveys (15%), Bitcoin's market share (10%), and Google search trends (10%).

data.title: Mining company Bitdeer's Bitcoin holdings increased to 833
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Nasdaq-listed Bitcoin mining company Bitdeer officially updated its Bitcoin holdings data, disclosing that its latest holdings as of February 14 were 833 BTC (excluding customer deposits), an increase of 28 BTC compared to last week.

data.title: Data: 24 LIBRA traders lost more than $1 million, and 61 traders lost more than $500,000
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to lmk.fun monitoring, a total of 24 traders lost more than $1 million on LIBRA, and 61 traders lost more than $500,000. The largest loser realized a loss of $5.17 million. He spent a total of $5.6 million to buy 2.1 million tokens, and finally sold them for only $430,000.

data.title: Smart money addresses once again deposited 2 million OM to Binance, equivalent to approximately $15.14 million
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: According to the monitoring of on-chain analyst Ai Yi, the smart money 0x177...4fe76 address that had previously recharged 8.71 million OMs has recharged 2 million OMs to Binance again, worth 15.14 million US dollars. According to the current price, if sold, it will make a profit of 14.68 million US dollars (cost 0.2261 US dollars, recharge price 7.57 US dollars). Currently, the two addresses still hold 2.03 million OMs, worth 15.36 million US dollars.

data.title: Burwick, the law firm that previously sued Pump.Fun: Users who have suffered financial losses on LIBRA can contact us
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News: The US law firm Burwick Law published a form and said: "If you have suffered a loss of funds on LIBRA, please contact us to learn about your legal rights. Burwick Law represents thousands of clients who want to recover their cryptocurrency losses." 
Previously, two US law firms (including Burwick Law) sued Pump.fun, demanding that the platform delete tokens using its IP. Since then, Burwick Law has revealed that its lawyers have received threats of violence and have been subjected to human flesh searches and personal information exposed along with several plaintiffs.

data.title: A smart money deposited 8.71 million OM into Binance, and sold it to make a profit of 61.23 million US dollars
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to the monitoring of Ai Yi, an analyst on the chain, the smart money "0x177...4fe76" has held OM for more than a year. Today, it is suspected that it has reduced its position by 8.71 million tokens and made a profit of 61.23 million US dollars. The address withdrew 23.83 million tokens from Binance at an average price of 0.2261 US dollars on January 3, 2024, transferred 11.53 million tokens to the new address 11 months ago, and recharged some tokens into the exchange nine hours ago. OM has increased by 36% in the past 24 hours and 94% in the past month.
